expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
expectations.evidence: response.md: DZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref) — Z-height of the part's CM above the floor marker

expectations.text: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
expectations.evidence: response.md: VZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref) — Z-velocity of the CM

expectations.text: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
expectations.evidence: response.md: third argument is 0.0 — 'Contact activates when ZCM < 0 (i.e., part penetrates the floor)'

expectations.text: Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
expectations.evidence: response.md: IMPACT(..., 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1) — table confirms K=1.0E5, e=1.5, C=50.0, d=0.1

expectations.text: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: response.md CMD block: function = "IMPACT(DZ(...),", "VZ(...),", "0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)" — split across three quoted strings per Adams continuation rules
